focus: Project.close depth and runtime VM/RSS feasibility
noveltyJustification: The iteration adds concrete upstream CocoIndex close-depth evidence plus live negative measurement bounds from the sandboxed runtime.
summary: ManagedConnection.close closes the target sqlite3 connection, but Project.close remains shallow for App/Environment/core state and unsafe under F-022 close-under-write; runtime RSS capture was blocked, so F-023/F-024 stay P2 pending unsandboxed measurement.

title: ManagedConnection.close releases the one target SQLite/sqlite-vec connection, but Project.close is still shallow because App, Environment, context provider, and CocoIndex core state survive until Project references are dropped
ManagedConnection.close does not acquire the RWLock or mark closed, so Project.close is unsafe as a concurrent close-under-write primitive
Runtime RSS unavailable; vm_stat snapshots show no pageout/swapout/compressor growth during failed status/search attempts
result: ps and pgrep denied process enumeration; sysctl denied swapusage; ccc shim failed with missing tree_sitter; venv CLI failed on daemon.spawn-lock outside sandbox; vm_stat pageouts and swapouts were unchanged across the failed venv CLI attempts.
interpretation: No daemon RSS bound can be claimed; the only quantitative bound is that the failed attempts did not increase global pageouts, swapouts, or compressor-occupied pages.

note: The ccc executable on PATH is a pipx shim at /Users/michelkerkmeester/.local/bin/ccc -> /Users/michelkerkmeester/.local/pipx/venvs/cocoindex-code/bin/ccc and fails before daemon contact because that environment lacks tree_sitter. The packet-local venv has tree_sitter, cocoindex_code, and cocoindex, but cannot create the daemon spawn lock under /Users/michelkerkmeester/.cocoindex_code in the current sandbox.

title: No runtime RSS evidence from this sandbox supports escalating adapter lifecycle findings to P1; keep F-023/F-024 as bounded fixed-cost resident-memory risks pending unsandboxed daemon/sidecar RSS measurement
Project.close hardening should be sequenced with remove-project cancel safety: close is P2 as shallow lifecycle cleanup, but P1 when RemoveProject can invoke it under active indexing

hypothesis: Induced sidecar fallback/5xx should be tested in iteration 006
reason: Not safe or useful in this sandbox: daemon and sidecar process control are inaccessible, status/search cannot reach the daemon, and a successful induced failure would intentionally load the bundled CrossEncoder fallback into the daemon, creating the memory-heavy side effect under audit.

hypothesis: Current executor can provide daemon and rerank sidecar RSS snapshots across normal status/search
reason: Process listing is denied via ps and pgrep, ccc on PATH fails before daemon contact with missing tree_sitter, and the packet-local venv cannot create the daemon spawn lock outside the writable sandbox.
